question: A Spring Boot worker publishes a message to Camunda without setting a TTL on the publish command and without overriding any `camunda.client.*` property. How long does the message remain buffered, per the starter defaults?

options.text: Indefinitely, until a process instance correlates with it.

a.text: The properties reference lists `camunda.client.message-time-to-live` with a default value of `PT1H`, which is the default TTL used when no value is supplied on the publish command.

b.text: `PT10S` is the default `camunda.client.request-timeout` — the gRPC/REST request timeout, not the message TTL.

c.text: `PT5M` is the default job-worker `timeout` (`camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout`), unrelated to message TTL.

d.text: Messages do not buffer indefinitely; without an explicit TTL the starter applies `camunda.client.message-time-to-live`, which defaults to `PT1H`.

explanation: The Spring Boot starter property `camunda.client.message-time-to-live` provides the default TTL for messages published without an explicit value, and its default is `PT1H`. The request timeout (`PT10S`) and the worker timeout (`PT5M`) are different concerns.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
